subject: Cloud Computing
title: Edge Computing and Data
description: A 60-second lesson on Edge Computing and Data.

hook.text: A self-driving car generates 4 TB of data per day. Sending it all to the cloud is too slow. Process it at the edge.

buildup.text: Edge computing processes data close to where it's created: in factories, cell towers, stores, and vehicles.

discovery.text: AWS Outposts, Azure Stack, and Google Distributed Cloud bring cloud services to locations outside data centers.

twist.text: Edge doesn't replace the cloud — it extends it. Critical decisions happen locally; everything else syncs to the cloud.

climax.text: As IoT grows, more data will be processed at the edge than in centralized clouds. The shift has already begun.

punchline.text: Some data can't wait for the cloud. Edge processes it locally.

quiz.question: Why is edge computing necessary for self-driving cars?
options: The massive data volume and latency requirements demand local processing
Self-driving cars cannot connect to the internet
Cloud providers do not allow automotive data on their servers
